title: Bookmarks: Favorite Sites
drDigitalIntro: Bookmarks are your favorites — websites you visit often. When you find a site you like, click Bookmarks > Bookmark This Page or press Command+D. Safari saves the address so you can return with one click. You can organize bookmarks into folders (News, Shopping, Weather, etc.). Unlike history, which is automatic, bookmarks are websites you manually choose to remember.

playgroundTask.question: What does bookmarking a website do?

options.text: Downloads the website to your Mac
Saves it so you can find it quickly later
Sends a link to your friends automatically
Adds it to your Reading List

drDigitalSuccess: Bookmarks save sites for quick return visits. Think of them as sticky notes on your favorite web addresses.
drDigitalHint: The word 'bookmark' is a hint — just like marking a page in a physical book to find it again.
